description:
## Question

What exactly is a remembered Workspace and how is its catalog managed: canonical identity, duplicate path handling, MRU update rules, removal, deleted-directory recovery, persistence bounds, and basename/path presentation? Resolve the remaining domain terminology and behavior into a decision suitable for the final specification.

comments.text: HITL decision: retain a remembered Workspace whose path is missing or unreadable as an unavailable catalog entry. Do not silently remove it; it can be retried if the path returns or removed explicitly by the user.
HITL decision: do not add special symlink handling in this version. Catalog identity is the validated root path under ordinary path handling; symlink aliases are not canonicalized or deduplicated.

HITL decision: move a catalog entry to the MRU head only after it successfully validates and loads as the Current Workspace. Mere selection, adding through the picker, and failed validation or load retries do not change recency.
HITL decision: removing the Current Workspace clears Current Workspace and shows the empty chooser. Do not automatically switch to the next MRU entry.

title: Specify the remembered Workspace catalog
